title: Discharge Summary - CHF exacerbation
content: Chief complaint: progressive dyspnea and bilateral lower extremity edema. Assessment: acute decompensated heart failure (HFrEF), hypertension, type 2 diabetes. Hospital course: diuresis with IV furosemide, ACE inhibitor restarted, education on fluid restriction. Discharge: follow Cardiology in 7 days. No acute infection identified.

title: Discharge Summary - CHF exacerbation
content: Chief complaint: progressive dyspnea and bilateral lower extremity edema. Assessment: acute decompensated heart failure (HFrEF), hypertension, type 2 diabetes. Hospital course: diuresis with IV furosemide, ACE inhibitor restarted, education on fluid restriction. Discharge: follow Cardiology in 7 days. No acute infection identified.

name: BMP & BNP panel
text: Sodium 138, potassium 3.9, creatinine 1.1, glucose 142, BNP 890 pg/mL (elevated). Consistent with volume overload in known HFrEF.

title: CT Chest w/ contrast
content: Indication: cough and weight loss. Findings: 1.8 cm spiculated nodule in RUL, mediastinal lymphadenopathy. Impression: suspicious for primary lung malignancy; recommend pulmonary oncology referral and PET-CT staging.

title: CBC with differential
content: WBC 11.2, hemoglobin 12.4, platelets 245. Mild leukocytosis without neutrophilia. No anemia of chronic disease pattern.

title: ED Triage Note
content: CC: right lower quadrant pain. Exam soft, RLQ tenderness. Plan: appendicitis workup, NPO, fluids, surgical consult.

title: US Abdomen
content: Non-compressible, dilated appendix measuring 9 mm. Periappendiceal fat stranding. Impression: acute appendicitis.

title: Discharge - CAP
content: Admitted for community-acquired pneumonia. Treated with ceftriaxone and azithromycin. O2 weaned to room air. Discharged with oral antibiotics and PCP follow-up in 48 hours.

title: Respiratory pathogen panel
content: Influenza A/B negative, RSV negative, SARS-CoV-2 negative. No atypical pathogens detected on PCR panel.

title: CXR 2-view
content: Cardiomegaly, pulmonary vascular congestion, small bilateral pleural effusions. Consistent with CHF exacerbation. No focal consolidation.
